subject: Science
overall_learning_objectives: Explain the concept of health including physical, mental, and social well-being
Analyze the impact of lifestyle and environment on health
Differentiate between symptoms and signs of diseases
Classify diseases into communicable and non-communicable categories
Explain modes of disease transmission and prevention
Understand immunity and the role of vaccines in disease prevention
Analyze responsible use of antibiotics and the problem of antibiotic resistance

lesson_plan.title: Concept of Health and Well-being
topics_covered: Physical, mental, and social aspects of health
student_activities: Identify factors affecting their own health
Discuss situations where mental health affects physical health

lesson_plan.title: Healthy Lifestyle and Daily Habits
student_activities: Prepare a chart of healthy and unhealthy habits
Analyze their daily routine and suggest improvements

lesson_plan.title: Role of Environment in Health
teaching_strategy: Visual comparison of clean and polluted environments
student_activities: Observe surroundings and list environmental health risks
Suggest ways to improve community hygiene

lesson_plan.title: Understanding Diseases: Symptoms and Signs
student_activities: Classify examples as symptoms or signs

lesson_plan.title: Communicable Diseases and Their Spread
topics_covered: Examples such as malaria, cholera, and influenza

lesson_plan.title: Non-Communicable Diseases and Lifestyle Factors
topics_covered: Examples such as diabetes and obesity
Role of diet and physical activity
student_activities: Conduct a small survey on lifestyle habits
Discuss how lifestyle influences disease risk

lesson_plan.title: Immunity, Vaccination, and Responsible Antibiotic Use
student_activities: Prepare a list of vaccines given in childhood
Discuss why misuse of antibiotics is dangerous

teaching_aids: Charts showing disease transmission and immunity
teacher_tip: Encourage students to connect textbook concepts with real-life situations such as hygiene habits, vaccination drives, and lifestyle choices.
